codeReview.summary: Timeline view is largely complete, but navigation target detection is overly broad and can misclassify ordinary dotted text as a source path, causing incorrect tab navigation on entry click.

issues.description: SOURCE_PATH_PATTERN matches generic dotted tokens (e.g., "Node.js", "v1.2") as source references. Because Timeline.tsx treats any source match as clickable and calls onTabChange("sources"), non-source timeline entries can incorrectly navigate to Sources. Evidence: regex at line 19 and local reproduction (`'What is Node.js?'.match(SOURCE_PATH_PATTERN)` => "Node.js").

sharedStateObservations.observation: Timeline validation coverage does not check false-positive navigation cases for non-path dotted text in log details.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md VAL-TL-005 verifies wiki-page navigation only; no assertion verifies that entries without wiki/source references remain non-clickable or that source detection excludes tokens like "Node.js".

summary: Review failed due to a blocking navigation-classification bug: source detection regex is too permissive and can trigger incorrect Sources-tab navigation for non-source text.
